decisions.reasoning: Glacial Lance is a guaranteed OHKO on Rillaboom. Even though Rillaboom outspeeds, the priority is to secure the KO immediately to remove the threat and gain momentum.

Calyrex-Ice is at 29% and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz or Knock Off. Switching to Incineroar provides an Intimidate drop and a safe resist to the opponent's likely physical attacks, preserving Calyrex-Ice for a potential late-game cleanup.
The Incineroar mirror match is a momentum sink; using Parting Shot allows me to pivot into a healthier teammate while simultaneously debuffing the opponent's attack, maintaining control of the field.
Since we are in a speed tie and the opponent's Incineroar is likely to stay in to trade chip damage, Knock Off is the best play to remove their item and gain information while maintaining momentum.

Calyrex-Ice is at 35% and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz or even a combination of moves. Switching to Incineroar provides an Intimidate drop and a resistance to Incineroar's Fire-type attacks, preserving Calyrex-Ice for a potential late-game cleanup.
The Incineroar mirror is a stalemate; using Parting Shot allows me to pivot into a better matchup while simultaneously lowering the opponent's offensive stats to maintain momentum.
Incineroar is being threatened by Hurricane and Tornadus-Therian is likely to pivot or attack; switching to Flutter Mane provides a faster threat that can pressure Tornadus or force a switch.
I outspeed Tornadus-Therian and Moonblast is a guaranteed 2HKO. Since the opponent cannot OHKO me and I am ahead in the exchange, staying in to secure the KO is the optimal play.
I outspeed Tornadus-Therian and Moonblast is a guaranteed 2HKO, whereas their best move against me is a 3HKO. Staying in to secure the KO is the optimal play according to the priority list.
Tornadus-Therian is at 16% HP, meaning Moonblast is a guaranteed KO. Since I outspeed and can secure the kill this turn, attacking is the optimal play.

Flutter Mane is guaranteed to be OHKOed by Flare Blitz and cannot secure a KO on Incineroar this turn. Switching to my own Incineroar provides a safe pivot to resist the incoming Fire-type attack and utilize Intimidate.
Since both Incineroars resist each other's primary STAB moves and are in a speed tie, Parting Shot is the best way to gain momentum, lower the opponent's stats, and bring in a teammate safely.
Since we are in a speed tie and the opponent's Incineroar is likely to stay in to trade chip damage, Knock Off is the best play to remove their item and gain utility while maintaining momentum.
Flutter Mane is at 5% HP and will be OHKOed by Flare Blitz. Switching to Incineroar provides a safe resistance to Fire-type moves, allowing me to preserve my remaining team members.
Since both Incineroars are at similar health and neither can force a quick KO, Parting Shot allows me to pivot out safely, lower the opponent's stats, and bring in a fresh teammate to gain momentum.
Since we are in a speed tie and the opponent's Incineroar is at 81% HP, using Knock Off is the safest play to remove their item and chip them down while avoiding the recoil damage of Flare Blitz.

Calyrex-Ice is at 35% and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz or even a combination of moves. Switching to Incineroar allows me to Intimidate the opponent and resist their Fire/Dark coverage, preserving Calyrex-Ice for a potential late-game cleanup.

Since both Incineroars are at a stalemate and Flare Blitz recoil would be disadvantageous, Parting Shot allows me to pivot out safely, lower the opponent's stats, and bring in a fresh teammate to gain momentum.

Since I am at 21% HP and the opponent's Flare Blitz does roughly 28%, I am likely to be KO'd regardless of my action. Using Flare Blitz maximizes chip damage on the opponent's Incineroar before I faint, which is the most productive play given my low health.

Flutter Mane is at 5% HP and will be OHKOed by Flare Blitz. Switching to Incineroar provides an Intimidate drop and a resistance to Flare Blitz, allowing me to survive the hit and potentially pivot or chip the opponent.

My Incineroar is at low HP and cannot win the 1v1 against the opponent's healthier Incineroar. Parting Shot allows me to safely pivot out, lower their stats, and bring in a fresh teammate to maintain momentum.
My Incineroar is at 16% and likely to be KO'd soon, so I should use Knock Off to remove the opponent's item before going down, as I cannot win a direct trade.
Flutter Mane has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Incineroar is the best defensive pivot to safely scout the opponent's next move while resisting potential Flare Blitzes.
My Incineroar is at 16% and will likely be KO'd by any move. Parting Shot allows me to pivot out safely, debuff the opponent, and bring in a fresh teammate to maintain momentum.
My Incineroar is at 10% HP and will likely be KO'd by any move, so I should use Knock Off to remove the opponent's item before fainting to provide utility for the rest of my team.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO and the best play to secure the KO on Incineroar before it can finish off Calyrex-Ice, as switching would likely result in losing momentum or taking unnecessary chip damage.
